Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Lorentzen Co. And Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Lorentzen Co. and Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Nichole M. Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Dominga Rodriguez, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Pedro Rodriguez, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Lorentzen Co. or Marine Engineering and Supply Company.

Default was entered against defendant Lorentzen Co. on November 3, 2017.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Lorentzen Co. was filed and served on November 3, 2017.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

Default was entered against defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company on May 8, 2017.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company was filed and served on May 8, 2017.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Dominga Rodriguez, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Pedro Rodriguez, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendants Lorentzen Co. and Marine Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Defendant Lorentzen Co:
Economic Damages: $651,203.18.
Non-Economic Damages - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $10,651,203.18.

Defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company:
Economic Damages: $651,203.18.
Non-Economic Damages - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $10,651,203.18.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Janine M. Fiel-Cosse of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Dominga Rodriguez as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Pedro Rodriguez, deceased

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 418.10
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tina Yim of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000, x234), appearing for defendant / moving party Sellers and Sellers Company, FKA Sellers Engineering
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Dominga Rodriguez, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death of Pedro Rodriguez

RULING:
DEFENDANT SELLERS AND SELLERS COMPANY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 418.10

Hearing held. The Court vacated its tentative ruling and took a brief break to review the courtesy copies submitted by the moving party at the hearing. After reviewing the documents the Court granted Defendant Sellers and Sellers Company's motion to quash service of summons pursuant to C.C.P. 418.10. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Jennifer Roux of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11033 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)
